body:
## Summary
Two BACKLOG §E follow-ups + D-1 closeout doc.

### E.1 — framework-tile drill-down
Framework tiles on `/ai` now link to `/findings?framework=<key>` (label = primary action). The external source-doc link demotes to a small ↗ icon on the right so customers don't lose the citation. Mirrors the drill-down the Dashboard `FrameworkCard` already had. The mapping-disclaimer tooltip stays on hover.

### E.2 — empty-state copy trim
"No identifiable AI users yet" copy was telling users about Entra ID P1/P2 inline. Slice 2.1's `/connect` banner now covers that — trimmed the inline note to cross-link to `/connect`.

### D-1 closeout (BACKLOG.md)
Verified the "`findings` table inconsistency" was a **misread of the data**, not a bug. The 5 "broken" backend@ scans had ALL their findings reparented to later scans via `_insert_finding`'s `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` clause. `scan_id` tracks "which scan most recently touched this finding," not "which scan discovered it." Proof: `c660c70b` has 39 of its 116 rows with `first_seen` BEFORE `b3091a57` even started — they were inherited from earlier scans.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm test` — **124 passed** (AISummary tests rewrapped in `MemoryRouter` for the new `<Link>`)
- [x] `pnpm exec tsc -b --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `pnpm build` — succeeds (1.2 MB bundle, ~367 KB gzipped)
- [x] Web bundle deployed: `aws s3 sync dist/ s3://ciso-copilot-app-470226123496/ --delete` + CloudFront invalidation `I29MJ8Y1PSCSLO1Q1SE567K7OA`
- [ ] **KK browser verify on `https://shasta.transilience.cloud/ai`** after CloudFront propagates: clicking a framework tile label navigates to `/findings?framework=<key>`; clicking the ↗ icon opens the source doc in a new tab; the mapping-disclaimer still shows on hover; empty-state copy on the Top AI Users section no longer mentions P1/P2 inline (replaced with link to `/connect`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(web): /ai framework-tile drill-down + cleaner empty-state copy

body:
## Summary
- **Task #54 — NIST AI 600-1 taxonomy alignment.** Rewrites all 12 Shasta `GAI-N` IDs to canonical `NIST.AI.600-1:2.N` section anchors per CME-v2 D-2 ("canonical published format is binding"). NIST AI 600-1 v1.0 does not use GAI-N — that was Shasta shorthand. Adds the 2 NIST §2 risks no GAI-N maps to today: **§2.1 (CBRN Information)** and **§2.10 (Intellectual Property)**.
- **Task #53 — MITRE ATLAS label drift.** Converts `mitre_atlas.control_descriptions` values from plain strings to `{name, description}` objects so consumers display canonical v4 technique short-names (`AML.T0010` = "AI Supply Chain Compromise") instead of trusting potentially-drifted Shasta check titles. ATLAS IDs themselves were already canonical; this fixes the label drift without ID rewrites.

## Mapping locked in

| Shasta | Canonical NIST AI 600-1 | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| GAI-1 Confabulation | `:2.2` | |
| GAI-2 Data Privacy | `:2.4` | |
| GAI-3 Bias Amplification | `:2.6` | Shasta splits NIST §2.6 into GAI-3+GAI-4 |
| GAI-4 Homogenisation | `:2.6` | |
| GAI-5 Harmful Content | `:2.3`, `:2.11` | NIST splits across two sections |
| GAI-6 Data Poisoning | `:2.9`, `:2.12` | Shasta extension — not a NIST §2-level risk |
| GAI-7 Prompt Injection | `:2.9` | Shasta extension — mentioned under §2.9 |
| GAI-8 Information Leakage | `:2.8`, `:2.9` | |
| GAI-9 Value Chain | `:2.12` | |
| GAI-10 Model Theft | `:2.9` | |
| GAI-11 Human-AI Config | `:2.7` | |
| GAI-12 Environmental | `:2.5` | |
| _(no GAI mapping)_ | `:2.1` | **CBRN Information or Capabilities** — added |
| _(no GAI mapping)_ | `:2.10` | **Intellectual Property** — added |

## Why now
KK's call after CME-v2 shipped: "only #53 and #54 from this session." Closes the "Shasta got the framework taxonomy slightly wrong" tail without expanding scope into #41 Findings History (deferred to fresh session).

## Notes
- Existing Aurora rows tagged `GAI-N` stay `GAI-N` until next purge — per KK's "don't backfill; we purge periodically pre-prod" stance from the CME-v2 spec.
- Removed a stray `"GOVERN 1.6"` entry that had been misplaced in `nist_ai_600_1.control_descriptions` (belongs only in `nist_ai_rmf`).
- All 4 scanner build.sh scripts (AWS / Azure / GCP / Entra / ai_scanner) copy from `scanner_core/` at image-build time, so next scanner image rebuild picks up the new registry.

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest platform/lambda/scanner_core/tests/` — **70 passed** (was 67; added 3 new tests covering canonical rewrite, §2.1+§2.10 registry presence, ATLAS dict shape).
- [x] `pytest platform/lambda/shasta_runner_entra/app/tests/` — 12 passed.
- [x] `pytest platform/lambda/ai_summary/tests/` — 2 passed.
- [x] `pytest platform/lambda/findings_list/tests/` — 4 passed (with DB env-var stubs).
- [x] End-to-end smoke: `fr.apply({...GAI-2,GAI-8...})` produces `[NIST.AI.600-1:2.4, NIST.AI.600-1:2.8, NIST.AI.600-1:2.9, NIST.AI.600-1:2.12]` (+ baseline rule additions); counters track normalize work; provenance records the firing rule.
- [ ] **Post-merge**: KK rescan (AWS Medium) to confirm new findings carry canonical IDs in Aurora `findings.frameworks` JSONB; `/findings?framework=nist_ai_600_1` chip continues to populate post-rewrite.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(registry): canonical NIST AI 600-1 §2.N IDs + structured ATLAS technique names

body:
## Summary

CME-v2 Slice 4 — final slice. Three pieces:

1. **AWS scanner image rebuilt + pushed** (S4.T1) — Fargate next-scan auto-pulls `:latest` which now carries the S2 rewrite tables + S3 baseline rules.
2. **Backend exposes `frameworks_meta`** (S4.T3) in both `ai_summary` and `compliance_summary` responses, carrying `{name, family, source_url, version}` per framework key.
3. **Frontend family-grouped tiles + mapping-not-attestation tooltips** (S4.T4 + S4.T5) on `/ai`, Dashboard, and `/findings` framework filter chip.

## Architectural commitments cashed in

- **D-4 (frameworks declare family)** is now operational end-to-end: registry → API response → UI grouping.
- **§14.1 (mappings, not attestations)** — every framework tile + filter chip carries `"Mapping only — not a compliance attestation. Verify with your auditor."` per spec language.
- **D-8 + D-10 (engine never branches on family)** preserved — engine is unchanged; family-aware logic lives entirely in UI + the read-side Lambdas.
- **§17.1 forward-compat** preserved — adding a privacy family later is a `framework_meta.py` edit; no engine or UI structural change required.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `platform/lambda/ai_summary/framework_meta.py` (new) | Per-framework metadata dict (8 AI + 9 security + 2 industry = 19 frameworks) |
| `platform/lambda/ai_summary/main.py` | Response includes `frameworks_meta` (AI-family only) |
| `platform/lambda/ai_summary/tests/conftest.py` (new) | sys.path + dummy DB env vars for tests |
| `platform/lambda/ai_summary/tests/test_main.py` | Assertions for new `frameworks_meta` field |
| `platform/lambda/compliance_summary/framework_meta.py` (new) | Same dict, copied (build-phase pragmatism) |
| `platform/lambda/compliance_summary/main.py` | Response includes `frameworks_meta` (all 19) |
| `web/src/lib/api.ts` | `FrameworkMeta` type + `frameworks_meta` field on both response interfaces |
| `web/src/routes/AISummary.tsx` | Tiles grouped under "AI frameworks" subhead; labels + source links from `frameworks_meta`; tooltip on each tile; stale "(S2)" annotations dropped |
| `web/src/routes/Dashboard.tsx` | Tiles grouped by family (security/ai/industry/privacy); tooltip + meta-based labels |
| `web/src/routes/TopRisks.tsx` | Framework filter chip carries disclaimer in `title` attr |
| `web/src/routes/AISummary.test.tsx` | Mock updated + new tests for family subhead + tooltip count |

## Test plan

- [x] `ai_summary` tests pass (2/2)
- [x] `AISummary.test.tsx` vitest passes (4/4 — 2 existing + 2 new)
- [x] `pnpm build` clean (tsc + vite, no new errors)
- [x] AWS scanner image pushed to ECR with new digest (sha256:510a13bc5903…)
- [x] Backend hotswapped: AiSummaryFn + ComplianceSummaryFn both updated successfully (7s)
- [x] Web bundle synced to S3 + CloudFront invalidation queued (`IGVWNSSFLBM0529JXU3V7W0LR`)
- [ ] **KK browser-verify** at `https://shasta.transilience.cloud`:
   - `/ai` shows tiles under an "AI FRAMEWORKS" subhead; hovering shows the disclaimer; labels link to source docs
   - Dashboard shows family-grouped tiles (security frameworks together, AI frameworks together, etc.)
   - `/findings?framework=eu_ai_act` chip carries the disclaimer on hover
- [ ] **KK rescan**: trigger AWS Medium scan; verify the 10 new baseline rules fire in CloudWatch + `findings.frameworks` carry canonical IDs

## CME-v2 status

| Slice | Status |
|---|---|
| S1 — engine | ✅ merged (PR #17) |
| S2 — rewrite tables (5 frameworks) | ✅ merged (PR #18) |
| S3 — canonical rules + baseline | ✅ merged (PR #19) |
| **S4 — deploy + web** | **✅ open (PR #20 — this)** |

After merge, CME-v2 is **complete**. The architecture supports:
- Future Findings History sub-project (§17.1) without engine changes
- Future Evidence Ingestion sub-project (§17.2) without engine changes
- New frameworks via JSON + framework_meta.py mirror entries
- New scanner sources via the standard commit-path hook pattern

## Known follow-ups (tracked separately)

- Shasta MITRE ATLAS label drift correction (Task #53)
- Shasta NIST AI 600-1 taxonomy alignment (Task #54)
- Findings History sub-project (Task #41)
- Evidence Ingestion sub-project (Task #42)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(cme-v2): Slice 4 — deploy + family tiles + mapping disclaimers (CME-v2 complete)

body:
## Summary

CME-v2 Slice 3 — two pieces of work in `ai_framework_registry.json`:

1. **Fix Slice E's nist_ai_600_1 format leak** — 3 rules incorrectly used RMF subcategory format (`GOVERN 1.6`) in the `nist_ai_600_1` field. Replaced with proper GAI-N risk identifiers.
2. **Author baseline registry rules** for 10 AI-domain check_ids that Shasta tags sparsely. Highest-priority fill: `sagemaker-notebook-root-access` went from 2 frameworks (soc2+fedramp) to 7.

## Part 1 — Slice E rule corrections

Each of 3 `ai_signin_*` rules had `nist_ai_600_1: ["GOVERN 1.6"]` (RMF subcategory in a 600-1 list). Rationale for new GAI-N mappings:

| Rule | New `nist_ai_600_1` | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `ai_signin_personal_tier_controls` | `GAI-2`, `GAI-8`, `GAI-9` | Data Privacy + Information Integrity + Third-Party — personal-tier AI use carries all three risks |
| `ai_signin_corp_tier_controls` | `GAI-9` only | Third-party AI is managed/sanctioned; privacy + leakage risks lower |
| `ai_signin_unknown_tier_controls` | `GAI-2`, `GAI-8`, `GAI-9` | Same as personal-tier (ambiguous case treated conservatively) |

Slice E integration tests updated. `GOVERN 1.6` kept in `nist_ai_600_1.control_descriptions` as a tombstone documenting the historical mistake.

## Part 2 — 10 baseline rules

Authored one rule per check_id, filling Shasta's framework gaps with canonical-format control IDs:

| Rule ID | check_id | Frameworks filled |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline_bedrock_content_filter` | bedrock-content-filter | nist_ai_rmf, iso_42001 |
| `baseline_bedrock_vpc_endpoint` | bedrock-vpc-endpoint | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act, iso_42001 |
| `baseline_cloudtrail_ai_events` | cloudtrail-ai-events | iso_42001, nist_ai_600_1, owasp_llm_top10 |
| `baseline_s3_training_data_versioned` | s3-training-data-versioned | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act |
| `baseline_sagemaker_notebook_root_access` | sagemaker-notebook-root-access | **full fill** — was soc2+fedramp only |
| `baseline_sagemaker_training_vpc` | sagemaker-training-vpc | **full fill** — was soc2+fedramp only |
| `baseline_sagemaker_model_approval` | sagemaker-model-approval | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act, nist_ai_600_1, owasp_llm_top10 |
| `baseline_sagemaker_endpoint_encryption` | sagemaker-endpoint-encryption | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act, nist_ai_600_1, owasp_llm_top10 |
| `baseline_sagemaker_data_capture` | sagemaker-data-capture | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act, nist_ai_600_1, owasp_llm_top10 |
| `baseline_sagemaker_model_registry_access` | sagemaker-model-registry-access | nist_ai_rmf, eu_ai_act, nist_ai_600_1, owasp_llm_top10, mitre_atlas |

Also: added `iso_42001.control_descriptions` for `A.6.2.4` (AI safety/robustness) and `A.8.2` (record-keeping) — previously empty.

## What S3 deliberately doesn't touch

- **Comprehensive check_ids** (`bedrock-guardrails-configured`, `bedrock-model-invocation-logging`, `s3-training-data-encrypted`, `lambda-ai-api-keys-not-hardcoded`): already 9-framework coverage from Shasta. Adding baseline rules would only duplicate. Skipped.
- **`pci_dss` and `hipaa` cross-tagging** for AI findings handling cardholder/health data: requires resource-classification feature (per CME-v2 §17.2 evidence ingestion sub-project). Deferred.
- **The 3 deferred frameworks** (`soc2_ai`, `iso_42001` paywalled aspects, `owasp_agentic`): their rewrite tables remain empty. No new control_descriptions for them in S3 beyond what S2 set.

## Test plan

- [x] 57 framework_registry tests pass (47 from S1+S2 + 10 new baseline integration tests)
- [x] Slice E test assertions updated for GAI-N format
- [x] Every new canonical ID validated against existing rewrite tables / control_descriptions
- [x] Registry validates at cold-start

## Operational effect

On next AWS scan with the fixtures present:
- All 10 fixture-driven check_ids will now receive their full AI framework coverage in the `findings.frameworks` JSONB
- `/findings?framework=eu_ai_act` should populate (not stay at 0 like before — `Article 15` now appears on Bedrock + SageMaker findings)
- CloudWatch `registry_apply_summary.rules_fired_count` will show non-empty counts (10 new rule IDs firing)

## CME-v2 status after this PR

- S1: engine ✓
- S2: 5 rewrite tables ✓ (3 deferred per spec)
- **S3: canonical rules + baseline fills ✓** (this PR)
- S4: web (family-grouped tiles, /compliance route, mapping-not-attestation tooltips) — next

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(cme-v2): Slice 3 — fix Slice E format + baseline rules for AI check_ids

body:
## Summary

CME-v2 Slice 2 — populates `rewrite_rules` for 5 of the 8 framework blocks. Engine machinery from S1 now becomes operational: scanner-emitted IDs get normalized to canonical published format before storage.

**Three frameworks deferred** (out of scope per spec §3 D-1 / §16):
- `soc2_ai` (AICPA paywall — awaiting KK's auditor input)
- `iso_42001` (ISO paywall + Shasta's `AI-` prefix oddity needs separate review)
- `owasp_agentic` (taxonomy not yet stable)

## Five frameworks authored

Each rule is **source-verified** against the published document (per spec D-3); each rewrite entry carries a `source` citation auditors can trace.

| Framework | Rewrites | Source | Notable findings |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **mitre_atlas** (S2.F1) | 16 (identity passthrough) | MITRE ATLAS v4 (raw GitHub) | **5 Shasta technique LABELS are outdated** for v4. IDs valid; semantic intent has drifted. JSON stores canonical v4 names. Flagged separately for Shasta-side fix. |
| **owasp_llm_top10** (S2.F2) | 10 (with renumbering) | OWASP 2025 release | Major reshuffle 2023→2025: 2 `2→1` merges (LLM07+LLM08 → LLM06:2025; LLM04+LLM10 → LLM10:2025). |
| **eu_ai_act** (S2.F3) | 8 (`EUAI-N` → `Article N`) | Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 | **Caught a real bug**: `EUAI-52` in Shasta uses pre-final numbering. In OJ L 2024/1689, transparency obligations are **Article 50**, not Article 52. Rewrite correctly maps `EUAI-52 → Article 50`. |
| **nist_ai_rmf** (S2.F4) | 19 (function → all subcategories) | NIST AI 100-1 + NIST AIRC | 72 subcategories total. Conservative 1:N fan-out (broad-but-defensible). |
| **nist_ai_600_1** (S2.F5) | 12 (identity passthrough) | NIST AI 600-1 (2024) | **Bigger Shasta divergences than ATLAS**: GAI-N numbering doesn't match NIST order; 2 Shasta entries (Data Poisoning, Prompt Injection) aren't standalone NIST risks; 2 NIST risks (CBRN, IP) have no Shasta coverage. control_descriptions store canonical NIST names. |

## Total

- **65 rewrite_rules entries** across 5 frameworks
- **~80 new control_descriptions entries** (5 frameworks × ~16 controls each on average)
- **+314 LOC** (registry JSON +198, tests +125)

## Test plan

- [x] 47 framework_registry tests pass (37 from S1 + 10 new S2 tests across 5 frameworks)
- [x] Registry validates at cold-start
- [x] Every rewrite rule carries a source citation (D-3 audit-defensibility commitment)

## Operational effect once deployed

Today's Shasta-emitted findings will be normalized on next scan:
- All `EUAI-N` tags become `Article N` (with `EUAI-52` → `Article 50` correction)
- All `LLMNN` tags become `LLMNN:2025` (with the 2 merges applied)
- All function-level `GOVERN-6` etc. expand to subcategory lists
- `AML.TNNNN` and `GAI-N` stay as-is (canonical-as-decided), with rich control_descriptions populated

`/ai` tile counts will be unchanged (one finding still counts once per framework key), but per-control drill-downs (future UI) will see canonical IDs.

## Three follow-up tasks tracked

- Shasta MITRE ATLAS label drift correction (future slice — Shasta-side fix)
- Shasta NIST AI 600-1 taxonomy alignment (future slice — Shasta-side fix)
- Same pattern likely exists for other compliance modules (`compliance/security/*.py` if any) — worth a future audit

## Out of this PR

- The three deferred frameworks remain in the JSON with empty `rewrite_rules`. They surface canonical-format flow when populated.
- CME-v2 S3 will rewrite Slice E's 3 rules to use canonical IDs. S2 didn't touch those (rules block unchanged).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(cme-v2): Slice 2 — rewrite tables for 5 AI frameworks (source-verified)
